objective: Add Qwen3.6-35B-A3B to the local model roster in the Mac-appropriate MLX form while preserving existing local runtime, PII, and no-payload probe boundaries.
scope_boundary: Add the Mac-equivalent Qwen3.6-35B-A3B model ID as an on-demand MLX candidate.
Update runtime inventory metadata and tests so the candidate is visible without downloading or loading the model.
Document memory placement on the Mac M5 Pro 48 GB local runtime lane.
Keep Guardrail-LAM and MCP intent residency unchanged.
Preserve PII routing boundaries and fail-closed guardrail behavior.
out_of_scope: Downloading, installing, benchmarking, or serving Qwen3.6-35B-A3B.
Changing Society of Minds routing order or fallback authority.
Sending prompt payloads to local, Windows, or cloud model endpoints.
Changing Windows Ollama model placement or allowlists.
research_summary: The local runtime slice from issue #228 identifies this machine as a MacBook Pro Mac17,8 with Apple M5 Pro and 48 GB unified memory, with MLX available and no local Ollama CLI in this shell. The Mac-equivalent roster entry for Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B should therefore use an MLX quantized package. The selected candidate is mlx-community/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit, recorded as on-demand only with a conservative 24 GB budget. BF16 is too large for this lane and 8-bit is too tight once resident guardrail and intent models are considered.

sprints.name: Mac MLX Roster Entry
sprints.goal: Record the Mac-equivalent Qwen3.6-35B-A3B candidate without changing runtime authority.
tasks: Add mlx-community/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit to .lam-config.yml as an on-demand large Worker-LAM candidate.
Add worker_lam_large to runtime inventory memory budgets.
Document the memory placement and variant choice in the local model runtime runbook.
acceptance_criteria: The roster uses an MLX model ID appropriate for this MacBook setup.
The entry is on-demand only and does not displace resident guardrail or warm MCP intent models.
Runtime inventory reports the new candidate with a conservative memory budget.

sprints.name: Validation and Evidence
sprints.goal: Prove the metadata-only roster change preserves governance and PII boundaries.
tasks: Add a focused runtime inventory test for the Qwen3.6 large worker candidate.
Run runtime, compile, structured-plan, and focused governance docs validation.
acceptance_criteria: PDCA/R artifact records Plan, Do, Check, Adjust, and Review.

specialist_reviews.focus: Mac MLX model placement, no-payload inventory behavior, and unchanged PII boundaries.
specialist_reviews.summary: The change adds metadata and documentation only. It does not install, load, route to, or execute the new model.

alternate_model_review.reviewer: not required for metadata-only roster candidate
alternate_model_review.summary: This issue does not modify STANDARDS.md, routing order, PII policy, or autonomous execution authority. A separate alternate-family cross-review is not required for this metadata-only roster candidate.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Add Qwen3.6-35B-A3B as a Mac MLX on-demand model candidate and validate the metadata-only runtime inventory/docs change.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Run local validation and update issue #347 with AC results.

material_deviation_rules: If validation requires downloading or loading the model, stop and create a separate benchmark/install issue.
If PII routing boundaries need changes, stop and route through a standards/cross-review slice.
If Windows/Ollama placement is requested, stop and create a separate Windows host model inventory issue.
If the 24 GB budget proves inaccurate in live benchmark evidence, update the issue with a follow-up rather than changing runtime authority in this slice.
approved_by: operator instruction: proceed, follow repo rules and loop through PDCA/R, GitHub epic, ACs, e2e testing as final AC
